title: Cloth case binding — a classic hard-backed journal
sources: Douglas Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901) — full case construction in cloth
notes: Multi-day pattern. Sewn text block (long-stitch or kettle stitch on tapes), rounded and backed, cloth case made separately, cased in with PVA, nipped in press, pressed under boards overnight. Teaches the full case-binding workflow in cloth — the modern book form. Endpapers sewn as a folded pair; grain direction throughout.
